Apple tree on the grounds of Westminster Presbyterian Church, visible from the sidewalk. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
The tree is located at the exit of The Place restaurant parking lot on Ashland Avenue Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
In the park right between hoyt lake and deleware ave. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Here and all the way down deleware ave there are apple trees. they are mature and produce ample fruit Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Unpruned apple tree, very mature, produces a lot of fruit. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-September.

There is a black walnut tree in Delaware Park along the ring road between Colvin and Meadow Rd. In the late summer/fall you'll see the lime green walnuts scattered around it on the ground. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-October.
3 cornelian cherry trees next to the shelter Access: Public.
Falling Fruit record for Cornelian cherry dogwood.

Tons of large, very healthy berries, ripe as of the end of July/beginning of August. Made a little jam from them - came out more like applesauce. Very sour, so you have to add sugar. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-August.
The serviceberry (June berry, saskatoon berry) tree is inside the Buffalo Zoo grounds right near the Bison. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: June-July.
